place: Upper West Side, Manhattan, New York

facts.text: Upper West Side was once called Bloomingdale, covering roughly 96th to 110th Streets.
Columbia University beyond 116th shaped the neighborhood’s vibe, and the Historical Society and American Museum of Natural History turned Central Park West into a boulevard of enlightenment.
The Ansonia Hotel at 73rd and Broadway, built in 1904, was a Beaux‑Arts palace that hosted opera stars, athletes, and socialites.
The Bloomingdale branch of the New York Public Library on West 100th Street preserves the Upper West Side’s public archive of neighborhood history.
Free walking tours led by historian Jim Mackin guide visitors through historic Bloomingdale between 96th and 110th Streets.
The Upper West Side has served as a backdrop for countless films and television shows.
